overview: Blue Terror is a U/B control archetype that leverages card advantage and disruption to outlast opponents. By cycling through the library with Mental Note and Lorien Revealed, it discards opponent's threats while finding answers. Counterspell provides early interaction, setting up a grindy mid-to-late game where resource superiority dictates victory.
win_conditions: Outdrawing and outmaneuvering opponents through cumulative card advantage
Discarding key threats to clear the board and hand
Countering game-ending spells to stall until superior resources close the game

keep_criteria: Hands with early Counterspell or cheap disruption
Hands containing Mental Note or Lorien Revealed with lands
Hands with at least two lands and a way to interact
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking interaction or card draw
Hands with only lands or only high-cost cards
Hands missing early game answers to aggressive strategies

early_game: Play lands and hold Counterspell for opponent's early threats
Use Mental Note or Lorien Revealed on curve to discard opponent's hand while drawing into answers
Prioritize survival and hand disruption over early board presence
mid_game: Continue cycling with discard spells to find removal and counters
Target opponent's key threats with discard and counterspells
Establish a minimal board presence if necessary to apply pressure
late_game: Leverage card advantage to overwhelm opponent with answers
Use discard spells to strip opponent's hand of finishers
Stall with counterspells until superior resources or value engines secure victory

key_cards.name: Mental Note
key_cards.notes: Cast on curve to discard opponent's threats while drawing into answers; use sparingly when opponent has few cards

key_cards.name: Lorien Revealed
key_cards.notes: Provides similar utility to Mental Note with a lower discard requirement; ideal for cycling when opponent has a full hand

key_cards.name: Counterspell
key_cards.notes: Hold up for opponent's early threats or key plays; prioritize countering game-changing spells over minor threats

sequencing_tips: Cast discard spells before playing lands to maximize hand size for counters
Hold Counterspell until opponent commits to a key play rather than using it preemptively
Cycle Mental Note and Lorien Revealed alternately to maintain hand disruption pressure

matchups.advice: Prioritize early Counterspell and discard spells to strip aggressive threats; play lands first to ensure you can cast interaction.
Race the opponent's card advantage by discarding their answers and countering their finishers; avoid overcommitting to the board.
Use Counterspell on the key combo piece and discard spells to strip their hand; prioritize finding the right answer over playing lands.
